steps.title: Join two complete clauses
steps.content: The sentence first states the exhibition's goal, ending with "the 1950s." What follows is another complete clause: "given the collection's breadth of more than 120 photos, its efforts were largely successful." The two clauses need a proper joining structure.

steps.title: Avoid run-ons and comma splices
steps.content: A leaves the complete clauses with no connector, creating a run-on. C uses only a comma, creating a comma splice. D uses "and" without the comma needed between long independent clauses. Comma plus "and" correctly links them. The answer is B.
